news.title: PepsiCo Hands Northern Europe Bottling To Carlsberg What It Means For Investors
news.summary: PepsiCo (NasdaqGS:PEP) has chosen Carlsberg as its future bottling and distribution partner in Northern Europe. The agreement covers Denmark, Finland, and the Baltics and is set to take effect from 2029. Royal Unibrew will see its existing bottling and distribution agreements with PepsiCo in these markets come to an end. The new arrangement expands Carlsberg's role in handling PepsiCo's beverage p

news.title: Carlsberg gains more PepsiCo rights as Royal Unibrew contract ends
news.summary: Royal Unibrew said it "would have welcomed the opportunity to continue the partnership; however, it has not been possible to reach an agreement".

news.title: Carlsberg Replaces Coke With Pepsi In Northern Europe, Royal Unibrew Shares Drop 20%
news.summary: Carlsberg will become PepsiCo's bottler in Denmark, Finland, and the Baltics from 2029, ending Coca-Cola agreements and expanding its footprint to 14 markets.
